description: Coordinate the harness stages from prompt/case planning through scene layout, asset binding, runtime execution, artifact collection, verification, diagnosis, and dataset packaging.
physical_assumptions: Each stage has structured inputs and outputs.
Runtime failures must be represented as explicit artifacts, not silent fallbacks.
Verifier decisions should be based on runtime evidence rather than UI state.
Generated dataset packages must preserve lineage from prompt/case to artifacts.
One session may explore multiple attempts, but only one selected attempt is registered as its source run.
Formal condition matrices preserve exact input fingerprints and explicit condition labels.
verifier_rules: Each stage writes its machine-readable artifact.
No stage hides a failure behind fallback success.
Artifact schema versions are present and compatible.
Dataset package references only existing artifacts.
Comparison quorum counts selected source runs, not lighting attempts.
Condition matrices do not produce a cross-condition quality winner.
Selected runs are checked for condition and acquisition compatibility before active run-index registration.
repair_suggestions: Rerun from the failed stage using the previous artifact as input.
Inspect verifier_report.json and run_readiness.json before modifying runtime code.
Keep generated cases and runs out of public git commits.
Run separate iterator sessions for each declared condition and keep only the selected attempt from each.
